+{"id":"bd-dc9y5","title":"[FUSE] mknod for char/block devices (overlayfs whiteout prerequisite)","description":"Follow-up to bd-z01zm: dispatch_mknod in ffs-fuse currently rejects every non-regular file type with EOPNOTSUPP. Required for overlayfs-as-upper-layer parity (whiteouts use mknod with S_IFCHR + rdev=makedev(0,0)) and for general POSIX mknod compatibility (FIFOs, sockets, char/block devices).

Required:
- Add FsOps::mknod(parent, name, mode, rdev, uid, gid) -> InodeAttr trait method with default impl returning ENOTSUP.
- OpenFs::ext4_mknod: route through ffs_inode::create_inode with the full mode (no S_IFREG OR), then for char/block devices clear EXT4_EXTENTS_FL and encode rdev into extent_bytes[0..4] (old format) or [4..8] (new format) per ext4_inode.h. For FIFO/socket use mode bits only (no rdev needed).
- Update dispatch_mknod to forward S_IFCHR/S_IFBLK/S_IFIFO/S_IFSOCK to ops.mknod() instead of rejecting.
- Tests: mknod whiteout char device (rdev=0), readback via getattr returns S_IFCHR + rdev=0; mknod block device with rdev=makedev(8,1), readback returns rdev encoded matching device_number().
- Then exercise overlayfs via xfstests overlay/{006,037,069}.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:18:30.774015760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:18:30.774015760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-devbv","title":"add mounted-path E2E and ioctl-trace coverage for ext4 EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION","description":"Survey finding: `FsOps::get_inode_version` exists in `crates/ffs-core/src/vfs.rs`, `ffs-fuse` dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION`, and `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` has direct dispatch tests, but `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` has no mounted-path proof for the ioctl.

Scope:
- Add a mounted-path helper in `fuse_e2e.rs` that issues `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` against a real ext4 file over FUSE.
- Assert the ioctl-trace log records the command when the kernel forwards it, and soft-skip only when the kernel/VFS rejects before userspace dispatch.
- Verify stable value encoding and buffer-size/errno behavior against the mounted path, not only the unit-dispatch shim.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` if operator-visible coverage now exists.

Acceptance:
- At least one mounted-path `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` scenario exists with deterministic assertions.
- CI artifacts make it clear whether failures are transport-layer skips or FrankenFS regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:09.486713081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:04:21.391364637Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:04:21.389261856Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuse","ioctl","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-dhk8","title":"Add ext4 inline-data EOF read-boundary conformance","description":"No ready beads remain. Add conformance coverage for ext4 INLINE_DATA read boundaries using existing fixtures: reads ending at EOF, reads past EOF, and continuation-tail bounds should return exact bytes/empty output without over-read. Update coverage docs and validate via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:24:50.830366504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:30:40.624041635Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:30:40.621632836Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inline-data EOF/read-boundary conformance coverage and updated coverage totals; focused test plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","inline-data"]}
 {"id":"bd-dj2pm","title":"add mounted-path btrfs symlink occupied-destination EEXIST and non-directory-parent ENOTDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E proves symlink creation and readlink success, but the public refusal contract for an occupied destination name or non-directory parent is not frozen even though ffs-core already returns Exists and NotADirectory. Scope: add btrfs mounted-path probes that assert exact EEXIST for existing names and ENOTDIR for non-directory parents, with unchanged directory contents and no partial mutation. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes btrfs symlink refusal behavior for these operator-visible cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:18.497089634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:09:58.000960289Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:09:57.998183582Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-yz3c","title":"ffs-cli mkfs must not delete output image on mkfs.ext4 failure","description":"mkfs_cmd currently calls std::fs::remove_file(output) when mkfs.ext4 exits non-zero. That is a destructive failure mode and contradicts the repo no-deletion posture. Preserve the sparse image on failure, report its path in the error, and add a regression test proving the file remains on disk after a forced mkfs failure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:59:04.670330005Z","created_by":"CloudyPanther","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:14.685196676Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:14.684980537Z","close_reason":"Preserved mkfs output image on mkfs.ext4 failure, added failure-path regression test, and verified fmt/check/clippy plus targeted test via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-z01zm","title":"[FUSE] overlayfs lower/upper redirect parity for whiteouts + redirect xattr","description":"Bring ffs-fuse to overlayfs(5)-as-upper-or-lower parity: handle whiteout char devices (0,0), opaque-dir markers (trusted.overlay.opaque), redirect xattrs (trusted.overlay.redirect), and metacopy markers (trusted.overlay.metacopy).

Context:
- overlayfs uses character device with rdev=makedev(0,0) for whiteouts. lookup must surface a hidden negative dentry; readdir must skip the whiteout entry.
- trusted.overlay.opaque='y' on a directory means: ignore lower-layer entries inside it. ffs-fuse must propagate this xattr through ffs-xattr (already supports trusted.* namespace) AND consult it in readdir to filter.
- trusted.overlay.redirect='/abs/path' marks a moved directory. The lookup must follow the redirect when descending.
- trusted.overlay.metacopy is a marker that the file's data is in the lower layer; reads still come from the upper inode for metadata, but data must be redirected. For a non-overlayfs FrankenFS mount this just needs to round-trip the xattr correctly.

Acceptance:
- Mount FrankenFS as the upper of an overlayfs mount; create + delete files; the deletion produces a 0,0 char device on the upper layer that the kernel respects.
- Mount FrankenFS as the lower; opaque-dir + redirect xattrs round-trip via ffs-xattr without loss.
- xfstests overlay/{006,037,069} pass with FrankenFS as upper layer.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:26:21.395361679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:26:21.395361679Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-z01zm","title":"[FUSE] overlayfs lower/upper redirect parity for whiteouts + redirect xattr","description":"Bring ffs-fuse to overlayfs(5)-as-upper-or-lower parity: handle whiteout char devices (0,0), opaque-dir markers (trusted.overlay.opaque), redirect xattrs (trusted.overlay.redirect), and metacopy markers (trusted.overlay.metacopy).

Context:
- overlayfs uses character device with rdev=makedev(0,0) for whiteouts. lookup must surface a hidden negative dentry; readdir must skip the whiteout entry.
- trusted.overlay.opaque='y' on a directory means: ignore lower-layer entries inside it. ffs-fuse must propagate this xattr through ffs-xattr (already supports trusted.* namespace) AND consult it in readdir to filter.
- trusted.overlay.redirect='/abs/path' marks a moved directory. The lookup must follow the redirect when descending.
- trusted.overlay.metacopy is a marker that the file's data is in the lower layer; reads still come from the upper inode for metadata, but data must be redirected. For a non-overlayfs FrankenFS mount this just needs to round-trip the xattr correctly.

Acceptance:
- Mount FrankenFS as the upper of an overlayfs mount; create + delete files; the deletion produces a 0,0 char device on the upper layer that the kernel respects.
- Mount FrankenFS as the lower; opaque-dir + redirect xattrs round-trip via ffs-xattr without loss.
- xfstests overlay/{006,037,069} pass with FrankenFS as upper layer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:26:21.395361679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:18:56.066209965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T11:18:56.064343993Z","close_reason":"trusted.overlay.{opaque,redirect,metacopy} now round-trip end-to-end through OpenFs::setxattr/getxattr/listxattr/removexattr. Root cause was ext4_setxattr building the XattrWriteAccess with has_cap_sys_admin=false even though the FUSE default_permissions mount means the kernel has already vetted the caller; switched to has_cap_sys_admin=true / has_cap_fowner=true. 4 new regressions cover the three xattrs + remove. Whiteout mknod (S_IFCHR + rdev=0) deferred — dispatch_mknod currently rejects every non-regular type with EOPNOTSUPP, tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-z0p6m","title":"port ext4 group descriptor checksum v2/v3 parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T04:57:55.842859811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:30:01.750721036Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T05:30:01.748447885Z","close_reason":"implemented ext4 group descriptor checksum v2/v3 parity and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-z0rn9","title":"CONFORMANCE: MVCC WAL record format golden round-trip harness","description":"# Family C (shipped 2026-04-24, closed in place)

Freezes the MVCC WAL header + commit record layouts owned by `ffs-mvcc::wal` via 20 golden-fixture tests in `crates/ffs-mvcc/tests/conformance_wal_golden.rs`:

## Header (16 bytes fixed)
- Bit-exact golden (magic 0x4D56_4357, version 1, csum_type=CRC32C).
- Magic/version/csum-type mismatch rejection.
- Short-payload rejection.

## Commit record (variable)
- Zero-writes minimum record (MIN_COMMIT_RECORD_SIZE = 29).
- Single-write with 64-byte payload + explicit byte-length arithmetic assertion.
- Multi-write with ordering preservation.
- Zero-length payload per write (kernel JBD2 revoke-block analogue).
- u64 bounds on CommitSeq/TxnId/BlockNumber.
- `commit_byte_size` contract across 0..5 writes; returns None for <4-byte input.

## Corruption detection
- CRC mismatch → Corrupted.
- Truncated mid-record → NeedMore(full_size).
- Zero record_len with non-zero tail → Corrupted.
- Empty input → EndOfData.
- All-zero tail → EndOfData.
- record_len below minimum → Corrupted.
- Unknown record type → Corrupted.
- num_writes past body capacity → early Corrupted (DoS guard).

Run: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_ffs_mvcc_cc_conformance cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --test conformance_wal_golden`.

Divergences at `crates/ffs-mvcc/tests/DISCREPANCIES.md` (3 ACCEPTED: CRC32C-only, COMMIT-only record type, all-zero-tail EndOfData sentinel).

Added `crc32c` to dev-dependencies so the corruption-detection tests can re-stamp CRCs after mutating the record body.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T22:34:10.321008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T22:34:10.321008Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T22:34:10.321008Z","close_reason":"20 tests green via rch; fixtures inlined, DISCREPANCIES.md documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-z15u","title":"e2compr: implement single-indirect block pointer read-modify-write","description":"Implement write support for indirect block pointers (i_block[12] -> pointer block -> data). This is the first level of indirect addressing and covers logical blocks 12 through 12+ptrs_per_block-1 (for 4K blocks: 12 through 1035).

STEPS:
1. When logical_block >= 12 and < 12 + ptrs_per_block:
   a. Read i_block[12]. If 0, allocate a new block, zero-fill it, write i_block[12].
   b. Read the indirect block.
   c. Write the target pointer at the correct 4-byte offset within it.
   d. Write the indirect block back to disk.
2. For freeing: when all pointers in the indirect block become 0, free the indirect block itself and set i_block[12] = 0.
3. Update set_indirect_block_ptr to dispatch to this path when lb >= 12 && lb < 12 + ptrs_per_block.

ACCEPTANCE: Compressed write works for files up to 4MB (1024 clusters * 4 blocks * 4K = 16MB with shift=2).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:07.272650906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:28.119620954Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:28.119131667Z","close_reason":"Added write_block_ptr() with single-indirect allocation + read-modify-write. All callers updated. Max file size for e2compr writes extended from 48KB to ~4MB. 490 tests pass. Commit 18cd3d7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2compr","ext4","write_path"]}
